title: Timothée Chalamet Wins Golden Globes Best Actor for Marty Supreme
text: At the 2026 Golden Globes, Timothée Chalamet claimed his first Golden Globe trophy in the Best Actor - Musical or Comedy category for his role in Josh Safdie's ping-pong film "Marty Supreme." This marks his inaugural win after five previous nominations. Chalamet expressed gratitude toward his director, saying: "Thank you from the bottom of my heart... thank you for this portrait." He also acknowledged his fellow nominees, calling the category "stacked," and praised the film's co-writer Ronald Bronstein and ensemble cast including Odessa A'zion, Gwyneth Paltrow, and Tyler, the Creator. The actor made a lighthearted joke about thanking Kevin O'Leary from "Shark Tank," then became more sentimental: "I'd be lying if I didn't say those moments didn't make this moment that much sweeter for my parents, for my partner, I love you." This public acknowledgment echoed similar remarks he made at the prior week's Critics Choice Awards. Chalamet defeated nominees George Clooney, Leonardo DiCaprio, Ethan Hawke, Lee Byung-Hun, and Jesse Plemons. In "Marty Supreme," Chalamet portrays a ping-pong hustler pursuing greatness, requiring extensive training for his role.
